summary: The U.S. Senate passed a non-binding resolution calling on President Trump to end the war in Iran or seek explicit Congressional authorization for continued hostilities, following the defection of four Republicans. IDF forces conducted operations in southern Lebanon, targeting suspected Hezbollah operatives in the Ras Ali Tahir area, and also operated in the Kerem Shalom crossing area in Gaza. A senior ISIS leader, Ali Husayn al-'Ulaywi, was killed in a U.S. Central Command precision airstrike in northwest Syria on June 19.
key_points: The U.S. Senate passed a 50-48 non-binding resolution calling on President Trump to end the war in Iran or obtain explicit Congressional approval to continue fighting, following the defection of four Republican senators. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93187)

IDF Spokesman reported that IDF forces attacked two armed Hezbollah operatives identified in the Ras Ali Tahir area in southern Lebanon after they posed a threat to forces operating in the security zone. (Source: @idf_telegram/24431)
U.S. Central Command confirmed a precision airstrike in northwest Syria on June 19 resulted in the death of senior ISIS leader Ali Husayn al-'Ulaywi. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4256)

CBS News reported that U.S. soldiers injured in an Iranian drone attack on the Shuaiba port in Kuwait on March 1 dispute the Pentagon's characterization of their injuries as minor, with one senior NCO suffering shrapnel throughout his body, brain concussion, hearing and vision loss, and lung damage. (Source: @naya_foriraq/79806)

IDF Spokesman reported that IDF forces raided the Kerem Shalom crossing area following suspicion of a Hamas operative on the Gaza side; one unarmed suspect was arrested and no infiltration into Israeli territory occurred. (Source: @amitsegal/62499)

IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi stated that IAEA inspectors are expected to visit nuclear facilities in Iran as part of the memorandum of understanding signed by the two presidents, emphasizing that inspections will be conducted independently. (Source: @asafroz/50677)

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghhi spoke by phone with a senior Hamas official and stated that Iran's negotiating team raises the issue of ongoing aggression against Gaza in all international forums and talks with mediators and the American side. (Source: @asafroz/50680)

U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth announced the successful first test of the new 'Golden Dome' interception system, utilizing advanced directed energy technology and the Dynamic Defense Autonomous Defeat (DDAD) system to autonomously detect, aim, and destroy a series of incoming threats. (Source: @asafroz/50679)

sections.executive_summary: The U.S. Senate passed a non-binding 50-48 resolution demanding President Trump end the war in Iran or seek Congressional authorization, signaling domestic political pressure on Iran operations. IDF conducted simultaneous counter-terrorism operations in southern Lebanon against Hezbollah operatives in Ras Ali Tahir and in the Kerem Shalom crossing area in Gaza against a Hamas suspect. A U.S. Central Command precision strike in northwest Syria on June 19 killed senior ISIS leader Ali Husayn al-'Ulaywi.

points: The U.S. Senate passed a 50-48 non-binding resolution calling on President Trump to end the war in Iran or obtain explicit Congressional authorization for continued hostilities, following the defection of four Republican senators. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93187)

CBS News reported that U.S. soldiers injured in an Iranian drone attack on a U.S. military site at Shuaiba port in Kuwait on March 1 dispute Pentagon claims that nearly 90% of approximately 400 injured soldiers had minor injuries; one senior NCO, Rodney Berman, suffered shrapnel throughout his body, brain concussion, hearing and vision loss, and lung damage, yet was classified as 'not seriously injured.' (Source: @naya_foriraq/79806)

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araraghi spoke by phone with a senior Hamas official and stated that Iran's negotiating team raises the issue of ongoing aggression against Gaza, continuous violations, and continued acts of destruction in all international forums and talks with mediators and the American side. (Source: @asafroz/50680)

IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi stated that IAEA inspectors are expected to visit Iran's nuclear facilities because it is part of the memorandum of understanding signed by the two presidents, and that the agency will conduct inspections independently without needing supervision or help from others. (Source: @asafroz/50677)

Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson stated that no one can be deceived; Iran cannot enjoy a peaceful region as long as the U.S. military and interventionist tendency continues and its occupation proxy commits endless wars and genocide with complete impunity. (Source: @naya_foriraq/79812)

The U.S. Secretary of Energy claimed that the return of oil flow to normal conditions has been delayed due to mines planted by Iran in the Strait of Hormuz, while the U.S. stated it will guarantee oil flow through the strait even without an agreement with Iran. (Source: @SharghDaily/167934)

IDF Spokesman reported that after being identified near the security zone, IDF forces attacked two armed Hezbollah operatives who posed a threat to forces operating in the Ras Ali Tahir area in southern Lebanon; both the Air Force and ground forces struck the operatives to remove the threat. (Source: @idf_telegram/24431)

IDF Spokesman reported that earlier in the day, IDF forces raided the Kerem Shalom crossing area following suspicion of a Hamas operative on the Gaza side of the crossing; one unarmed suspect was arrested and transferred for interrogation by security forces, with no infiltration into Israeli territory. (Source: @amitsegal/62499)

IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir held a meeting with rabbis from the Zionist-religious yeshiva world, with senior IDF officials including the Deputy Chief of Staff, Ground Forces Commander, Chief Military Advocate General, and Chief Military Rabbi in attendance. (Source: @idf_telegram/24427)

During the meeting, the Chief of Staff emphasized the need for increased manpower to meet unprecedented operational challenges across all arenas of combat, clarifying that the IDF still lacks thousands of fighters and needs every fighter to fill tasks and consolidate operational achievements. (Source: @idf_telegram/24427)

The Chief of Staff stressed the importance of implementing the Joint Service Order, which allows service alongside rather than at the expense of others, and expanding the integration of women into key and combat positions in full alignment with the Joint Service Order in routine, emergency, and wartime. (Source: @idf_telegram/24427)

i24NEWS reported, with security source approval, that the U.S. military has begun diluting the presence of firefighting aircraft at Israel's Ben Gurion Airport at Israel's request to increase the airport's aircraft capacity ahead of summer. (Source: @inon_yttach/9143)

IDF Spokesman reported that earlier in the day, IDF forces raided the Kerem Shalom crossing area following suspicion of a Hamas operative on the Gaza side of the crossing; one unarmed suspect was arrested and transferred for interrogation by security forces. (Source: @amitsegal/62499)

An Israeli flash news report stated that a Bedouin IDF employee, Raad Ibrahim Salama, was killed in an operational accident in the Gaza Strip while performing engineering work in the Beit Lahia area; a mosque's minaret crane collapsed on the bulldozer, killing the operator at the scene. (Source: @pkpoi/142956)

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araraghi raised with a senior Hamas official the issue of the 'ongoing aggression of the occupiers against Gaza, the continuous violations, and the continuation of acts of destruction' in all international forums and talks with mediators and the American side. (Source: @asafroz/50680)

IDF Spokesman reported that after being identified near the security zone, IDF forces attacked two armed Hezbollah operatives who posed a threat to forces operating in the Ras Ali Tahir area in southern Lebanon; both the Air Force and ground forces struck the operatives to remove the threat. (Source: @idf_telegram/24431)

IDF Spokesman reported that a vehicle with suspects who crossed the security zone in southern Lebanon was identified in the Ras Ali Tahir area and posed a threat to IDF forces; after identification, the Air Force struck the suspects to remove the threat. (Source: @yediotnews25/131892)

IDF Spokesman stated that the IDF will not allow the Hezbollah terrorist organization to harm citizens of the State of Israel and its forces and will continue to work to remove immediate threats. (Source: @idf_telegram/24431)
U.S. Central Command confirmed that a precision airstrike in northwest Syria on June 19 resulted in the death of senior ISIS leader Ali Husayn al-'Ulaywi. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4256)

CBS News reported that U.S. soldiers injured in an Iranian drone attack on a U.S. military site at Shuaiba port in Kuwait on March 1 dispute Pentagon claims of minor injuries; the attack on the Shuaiba port military site resulted in the deaths of six U.S. military personnel and injuries to more than twenty others. (Source: @naya_foriraq/79806)

CBS News reported that U.S. soldiers injured in an Iranian drone attack on a U.S. military site at Shuaiba port in Kuwait on March 1 dispute Pentagon claims that most injuries were minor; medical records for one soldier, Corporal Corey Hicks, showed he suffered severe shrapnel injuries and underwent multiple emergency surgeries at a Kuwaiti hospital, and he is currently recovering from a traumatic brain injury. (Source: @naya_foriraq/79806)

CBS News reported that an Iranian drone attack on a U.S. military site at Shuaiba port in Kuwait on March 1 resulted in the deaths of six U.S. military personnel and injuries to more than twenty others at the American military site. (Source: @naya_foriraq/79806)

The U.S. Secretary of Energy claimed that the return of oil flow to normal conditions has been delayed due to mines planted by Iran in the Strait of Hormuz, while the U.S. stated it will guarantee oil flow through the strait even without an agreement with Iran. (Source: @SharghDaily/167934)

Russian Deputy National Security Council Secretary Dmitry Medvedev stated that recent developments in the Middle East have shown that foreign military bases created under the pretext of providing security to host countries are ineffective and instead make host countries targets for military strikes. (Source: @SharghDaily/167931)

sections.threat_assessment: The U.S. Senate's non-binding 50-48 resolution on Iran signals growing domestic political pressure that could constrain military operations, while IDF maintains active counter-terrorism operations across multiple theaters including southern Lebanon (Ras Ali Tahir) and the Kerem Shalom crossing. The continued Iranian drone capabilities demonstrated in the Shuaiba port attack and the claimed mining of the Strait of Hormuz indicate persistent asymmetric threats to U.S. forces and Gulf energy infrastructure. The death of senior ISIS leader Ali Husayn al-'Ulaywi in a U.S. Central Command strike in northwest Syria represents a counter-terrorism success but does not eliminate the broader regional threat landscape.
sections.regional_response: Iran continues diplomatic engagement with Hamas through a phone call between Foreign Minister Araraghi and a senior Hamas official, raising the issue of Gaza aggression in international forums. Russia's Deputy National Security Council Secretary Medvedev publicly criticized foreign military bases in the Middle East as ineffective and counterproductive for host countries. The IAEA under Director General Grossi is moving toward inspections of Iranian nuclear facilities under the existing memorandum of understanding, with Grossi emphasizing the agency's operational independence.
sections.intelligence_notes: The IDF Chief of Staff's meeting with religious Zionist rabbis highlights ongoing domestic Israeli discussions about military service integration, particularly regarding yeshiva and mechina students, women in combat roles, and implementation of the Joint Service Order. The dilution of U.S. firefighting aircraft presence at Ben Gurion Airport, reported by i24NEWS with security source approval, appears to be a logistical adjustment for summer capacity rather than a force reduction. The discrepancy between Pentagon injury classifications and actual medical conditions among U.S. soldiers from the Shuaiba attack warrants continued monitoring for force readiness implications.
